key_risks: A sharp tech/semiconductor momentum reversal or factor rotation into value/defensives could hit the concentrated growth tilt hard.
Disappointing June 1 ISM Manufacturing or June 3 ISM Services prints could trigger a risk-off move dragging high-beta tech.
Rising long-term yields (30Y at 5.01%) could pressure high-multiple growth names within the window.
Taiwan and semis carry geopolitical and currency risk that could cause sudden drawdowns.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest near-term momentum (+5.6% 7d, +22% 30d), robust chip sales data up 79% YoY, and AI capex tailwind; leadership likely persists into June 4.
Broad tech leadership (+4.6% 7d) supported by strong software pricing and AI infrastructure spending; less concentrated than pure semis.
Momentum factor leading (+4.1% 7d) in a low-VIX, tight-credit risk-on tape; trend persistence favored over a one-week window.
Mega-cap growth exposure with strong trend (+2.95% 7d) and beta to continued tech leadership.
Semiconductor supply-chain proxy with explosive momentum (+8.3% 7d); leverages same AI/chip cycle driving leadership.

portfolio_rationale: Risk-on backdrop with VIX at 16, tight credit spreads, Fed on hold, and powerful AI/semiconductor momentum argues for an overweight to tech leadership for the one-week window while keeping diversification within the theme.
rationale_summary: Concentrate in semiconductor and tech momentum leaders that show the strongest short-term trend and fundamental tailwinds heading into June 4.
